author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John v. 2, ‘Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John v. 2, ‘Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Gospel has just sounded in our ears, and made us intent to know what is the meaning of what has been read.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This, I suppose, is looked for from me, this I promise, by the Lord’s assistance, to explain as well as I can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For without doubt it is not without a meaning, that those miracles were done, and something they figured out to us bearing on eternal saving health.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the health of the body which was restored to this man, of how long duration was it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what is your life?” saith Holy Scripture; “it is a vapour that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in that health was restored to this man’s body for a time, some enduringness was restored to a vapour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then this is not to be valued much; “Vain is the health of man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, brethren, recollect that Prophetical and Evangelical testimony, for it is read in the Gospel; “All flesh is grass, and all the glory of flesh as the flower of grass; the grass withereth, the flower falleth away, the Word of the Lord endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word of the Lord communicateth glory even to the grass, and no transitory glory; for even to flesh He giveth immortality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But first passeth away the tribulation of this life, out of which He giveth us help, to whom we have said, “Give us help from tribulation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And all this life is indeed a tribulation to the understanding.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there are two tormentors of the soul, torturing it not at once, but alternating their tortures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These two tormentors’ names are, Fear and Sorrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When it is well with thee, thou art in fear; when it is ill, thou art in sorrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This world’s prosperity, whom doth it not deceive, its adversity not break?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this grass, and in the days of grass, the surer way must be kept to, the Word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when it had been said, “All flesh is grass, and all the glory of flesh as the flower of grass, the grass withereth, the flower falleth away;” as though we should ask, “What hope has grass? what stability the flower of grass?” it is said, “but the Word of the Lord endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And whence, you will say, is that Word to me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Word was made Flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Word of the Lord saith to thee, “Do not reject My promise, for I have not rejected thy grass.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then that the Word of the Lord hath granted to us, that we might hold to Him, that we might not pass away with the flower of grass; this, I say, that He hath granted to us, that the Word should be made Flesh, taking Flesh, not changed into flesh, abiding, and assuming, abiding what He was, assuming what He was not; this, I say, that He hath granted to us, that pool also signifies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am speaking briefly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That water was the Jewish people; the five porches were the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Moses wrote five books.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore was the water enclosed by five porches as that people was held in by the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The troubling of the water is the Lord’s Passion among that people.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who descended was healed, and only one; for this is unity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever are offended at the Passion of Christ are proud; they will not descend, they are not healed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, say they, “Am I to believe that God was Incarnate, that God was born of a woman, that God was crucified, scourged, dead, wounded, buried?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it far from me to believe this of God, it is unworthy of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the heart speak, not the neck.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the proud the humiliation of the Lord seems unworthy of Him, therefore is saving health from such far off.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lift not thyself up; if thou wouldest be made whole, descend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well might piety be alarmed, if Christ in the flesh subject to change were only spoken of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now the truth sets forth to thee, Christ Unchangeable in His Nature as the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God;” not a word to sound, and so pass away; for “the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thy God endureth unchangeable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O true piety; thy God endureth, fear not; He doth not perish, and through Him, thou too dost not perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He endureth, He is born of a woman, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word made even His Mother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who was before He was made, made her in whom He was to be made Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was an infant, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sucked, He grew, He took nourishment, He ran through the several stages of life, He came to man’s estate, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was wearied, and He slept, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He suffered hunger and thirst, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was apprehended, bound, scourged, assailed with railings, crucified finally, and killed, but in the Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou alarmed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Word of the Lord endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso rejecteth this humiliation of God, doth not wish for healing from the deadly swelling of pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then by His Flesh did the Lord Jesus Christ grant hope to our flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He took on Him what we knew well in this earth, what aboundeth here, to be born, and to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be born and to die, abounded here; to rise again and to live for ever, was not here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Poor earthly merchandize found He here, He brought here strange and heavenly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art alarmed at death, love the resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath given thee help out of tribulation; for vain thy health had ever been.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us acknowledge therefore and love the saving health in this world strange, that is, health everlasting, and live we in this world as strangers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us think that we are but passing away, so shall we be sinning less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us rather give thanks to our Lord God, that He hath been pleased that the last day of this life should be both near and uncertain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From the earliest infancy even to decrepit old age, it is but a short span.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Adam had died to-day, what would it have profited him, that he had lived so long?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What “long time” is there in that in which there is an end?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one recalleth yesterday; to-day is pressed on by to-morrow, that it may pass away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this little span let us live well, that we may go whence we may not pass away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now even as we are talking, we are indeed passing away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our words run on, and the hours fly by; so does our age, so our actions, so our honours, so our misery, so our happiness here below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All passeth away, but let us not be alarmed; “The Word of God endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to the Lord, etc.
